text.content:
❧ Animalism

Level 1: Bond Famulus

Cost: Free (initial bonding costs 1 Rouse per night x3) | Pool: Cha + Animal Ken | Type: Utility

Feed the animal your blood on three separate nights to create a ghoul animal familiar. The Famulus is loyal, can be commanded telepathically within line of sight, and does not age. You can see through its senses by concentrating (1 turn). Only one Famulus at a time. If it dies, you can bond a new one after one lunar cycle.

Level 1: Sense the Beast

Cost: Free | Pool: Res + Animal Ken | Type: Utility

By focusing on a target within line of sight, sense whether they carry a supernatural Beast. Detects vampires, werewolves, and other creatures with a predatory aura. A critical win reveals the creature type; a regular success only confirms something inhuman. Does not work through electronic media. Lasts one scene.

Level 2: Feral Whispers

Cost: Free (1 Rouse to command swarms or multiple animals) | Pool: Man + Animal Ken | Type: Utility

Communicate telepathically with a single animal you can see. You can issue commands with a Manipulation + Animal Ken roll; the animal follows instructions to the best of its ability but interprets them in animal terms. Complex commands (guard this door, follow that person and return) require more successes. Lasts one scene or until the task is complete. Animals remember you favorably afterward.

Level 3: Animal Succulence

Cost: Free | Pool: -- | Type: Utility

Passive. Animal blood slakes Hunger more effectively — each drink from an animal reduces Hunger by 2 instead of 1. Additionally, you never risk a Bestial Failure when feeding on animals. This does not change the Hunger minimum (Hunger 1 can never be slaked by animals).

Level 3: Quell the Beast

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: Cha + Animal Ken vs Composure + Resolve | Type: Combat

Target a vampire or mortal within conversational range. On success, suppress their Beast — vampires cannot frenzy and mortals become docile and passive. The target feels drained and lethargic. Lasts for the scene or until the target takes damage. Contested by the target's Composure + Resolve. On a critical win, the target cannot frenzy for the rest of the night.

Level 3: Unliving Hive

Cost: Free | Pool: -- | Type: Utility

You can bond insects and other vermin as Famuli instead of larger animals. A swarm counts as a single Famulus. The swarm can scout, deliver messages, or attack (dealing 1 superficial damage per turn to a target they engulf). You sense through the swarm collectively. The swarm is fragile — fire or area effects destroy it.

Level 4: Subsume the Spirit

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: Man + Animal Ken | Type: Utility

Project your consciousness into your bonded Famulus, taking full control of its body. Your own body falls into a torpor-like state and is defenseless. You gain the animal's physical capabilities (flight, aquatic movement, etc.) while retaining your mental Attributes. If the animal is killed, you awaken in your body but take 2 Aggravated Willpower damage. Lasts until you choose to return or until sunrise.

Level 5: Animal Dominion

Cost: 2 Rouse | Pool: Man + Animal Ken | Type: Combat

Command all animals of a chosen type within approximately 1 mile. Every rat, dog, bird, etc. of that type obeys your will simultaneously. You can direct them to attack (swarm deals 3 Aggravated damage per turn), scout an area, or create diversions. Maintaining control requires concentration — you can take no other actions. Lasts one scene. Extremely conspicuous.

Level 5: Drawing Out the Beast

Cost: 1 Rouse | Pool: Wits + Animal Ken vs Composure + Resolve | Type: Combat

When you would enter frenzy, you can instead project your Beast into another being within line of sight. That target enters frenzy in your place with your Beast driving them — they attack and rage while you remain calm but emotionless (cannot use Willpower, feel no fear or anger). If the target is a vampire, they frenzy normally. If mortal, they go berserk. Lasts until the frenzy would naturally end. If the target is killed or leaves your line of sight, the Beast returns to you and you must immediately roll to resist frenzy at +2 difficulty.
